```{python}
    ax.set_xlabel('Percentage of Customers Targeted (%)', fontsize=12)
    ax.set_ylabel('Percentage of All Successes Captured (%)', fontsize=12)
    ax.set_title(f'{epoch}\nTop 10% Captures {success_10:.1f}% of Successes', 
                fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_xlim([0, 100])
    ax.set_ylim([0, 100])
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
    ax.legend()

plt.suptitle('Cumulative Success Capture by Epoch', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

#### 4.5 Key Findings

```{python}
for epoch in epoch_names:
    overall = test[test['epoch']==epoch]['y_numeric'].mean() * 100
    hit10 = epoch_lift_results[epoch][0.1]['hit_rate'] * 100
    hit50 = epoch_lift_results[epoch][0.5]['hit_rate'] * 100
    lift10 = epoch_lift_results[epoch][0.1]['lift']
    capture10 = epoch_lift_results[epoch][0.1]['percent_of_success'] * 100
    
    print(f"\n{epoch}:")
    print(f"  • Random targeting gives {overall:.1f}% success rate")
    print(f"  • Top 10% targeting gives {hit10:.1f}% success rate ({lift10:.2f}x lift)")
    print(f"  • Top 50% targeting gives {hit50:.1f}% success rate")
    print(f"  • Top 10% captures {capture10:.1f}% of all successful conversions")
```

|                      |
|:---------------------|
| KEY FINDINGS SUMMARY |

| Metric | Epoch 1 (19.0%) | Epoch 2 (53.2%) | Epoch 3 (49.6%) |
|-----------------------------------|------------|--------------|------------|
| Top 10% Hit Rate | 46.8% | 76.0% | 78.4% |
| Top 10% Lift | 2.46x | 1.43x | 1.58x |
| Top 50% Hit Rate | 27.5% | 67.5% | 65.9% |
| Success Capture (Top 10%) | 24.6% | 14.3% | 15.8% |

1.  ***THE MODEL WORKS BEST WHEN YOU NEED IT MOST***

During the crisis period when only 19% of people were subscribing, our model identified a group where 47% said yes—more than double the random rate. It's like finding gold nuggets in a desert. During recovery when half of people were already subscribing, the model still boosted success to 76-78%—turning good odds into near certainty.

2.  ***TARGETING THE RIGHT PEOPLE DRAMATICALLY BOOSTS RESULTS***

If you call 100 random people during a crisis, you'll get about 19 subscribers. But if you use the model to call the top 10% of prospects, you'll get 47 subscribers from those same 100 calls. That's 28 extra subscriptions without making a single additional call.

3.  ***THE MODEL MAKES MONEY IN ANY ECONOMIC CLIMATE***

Running the numbers: Let's suppose each call costs about $10 and each successful subscription brings $500 profit. Using the model instead of random calling adds approximately $14,000 extra profit per 100 calls during the crisis (Epoch 1), $11,500 extra profit per 100 calls during early recovery (Epoch 2), and $14,000 extra profit per 100 calls in late recovery (Epoch 3). These gains come from the model's ability to identify the most promising prospects turning 19 random successes into 47 targeted successes when success rates are low, and boosting already good success rates from 53% to 76% when economic conditions are favorable. Across all economic conditions, the model consistently delivers substantial value, proving that data-driven targeting pays for itself many times over whether the economy is booming or busting.

4.  ***HOW TO TARGET BASED ON OUR GOALS***

Want maximum efficiency? Target the top 10-15% of prospects. You'll get the highest success rates and best return on every call dollar. Want maximum volume? Target the top 40-50%. You'll capture 70-80% of all possible subscribers, though with slightly lower efficiency. Want balance? Target the top 25-30%. You'll get strong results without leaving too many potential subscribers on the table.

5.  ***WATCH THE ECONOMY TO GUIDE YOUR STRATEGY***

When Euribor rates drop below 1% (like during our Epochs 2-3), success rates naturally rise above 50%. During these times, you can confidently call more people. When rates are higher and success rates lower, rely heavily on the model to find the few willing customers.

-----------------------------------------------------------------
